description_long: This is a large, modern commercial jet airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a pair of long, swept-back wings. The aircraft features four underwing engines, two on each side, and a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The nose is rounded and the cockpit windows are positioned at the front of the fuselage, indicating a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retracted. The exterior is primarily a metallic gray with minimal markings, except for the number '741' on the tail fin. The overall design is typical of wide-body passenger jets used for long-haul flights.
description: A large, metallic gray commercial jet airplane with four underwing engines, swept-back wings, and a conventional tail. The cockpit is forward-mounted and the tail fin is marked with '741'.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full fuselage, swept-back wings, four engines, and the '741' marking on the tail.
description_view_1: Rear oblique view highlighting the tail assembly, wing sweep, and engine placement under the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the fuselage, wing structure, and engine configuration from the left.
description_view_3: Front oblique view emphasizing the nose, cockpit windows, and the full span of the wings with all four engines visible.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with long, swept-back wings and slight upward winglets at the tips.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two on each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage.
